_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists).
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Bridging of a gap in a tendon with an autogenous donor tendon from the same or a separate extremity

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission

claim.label: Post Procedure clinical photograph of affected part (of donor and recipient sites- for tendon graft)

ppd_preauth.q: Does the Clinical notes, clinical photograph of affected part justifying the need for the procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Does the Clinical notes, clinical photograph of affected part justifying the need for the procedure?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or clinical photograph of affected part do not justify the medical necessity of the procedure at pre-authorization.
Clinical notes at pre-authorization lack adequate detail regarding history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, or indication for the procedure.
Post-procedure clinical photograph for tendon grafting does not clearly show both donor and recipient sites.
Procedure / operation notes at claim submission lack sufficient detail on surgical steps or outcomes.

clinical_key_pointers: Tendon grafting involves the bridging of a gap in a tendon with an autogenous donor tendon from the same or a separate extremity.
Commonly used donor tendons include the palmaris longus, the plantaris, and, on occasion, a toe extensor.
One of the most important criteria for a successful tendon graft procedure is the establishment and maintenance of good passive motion of the involved segment and adjacent rays.
Splinting may be effectively employed for creating and preserving supple digits, both preoperatively and during the postoperative course.
Postoperative splinting should be designed to meet the individual problems presented.
